title: launaea acanthodes subsp. tomentella
intro: Launaea acanthodes subsp. tomentella is a subspecies of plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to North Africa and the Middle East. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Morocco, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing. Herbal preparations of Launaea acanthodes subsp. tomentella include infusions made from the leaves, which are typically consumed as a tea to reap its medicinal benefits.
